name: Herero
notes: Herero (Otjiherero), a Bantu language of Namibia and Botswana. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Latin alphabet (the OshiHerero alphabet, established by 19th-century missionary orthography). A full spec must model the dental vs alveolar stop and nasal contrast (/t̪ t/, /n̪ n/), the prenasalised series (ᵐb ⁿd̪ ⁿd ⁿdʒ ᵑɡ), the interdental fricatives /θ ð/, contrastive vowel length, and tone — the mapping from the missionary spelling to these is not one-to-one and no normative letter-to-IPA table was found. Input is assumed to be the standard Latin Herero orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode here1253), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.
